hook: Brand new vs resale landed: who's blinking first in April 2026?

slides.sub: Brand new vs resale — which sellers are cutting, where, and by how much this week.

slides.lead: Two landed markets are diverging sharply in April 2026.
slides.source: URA caveats · POV Guy analysis · Apr 2026

slides.pre: Typical resale landed discount to last ask
slides.post: Sellers accepting ~5% below final asking price — widest gap since mid-2023.

items.body: New launches (D10/D11 GCB-adjacent) priced firm at S$1,850–2,200 psf; developers won't budge on show-unit pricing.
Resale terraces in D15 and D19 sitting 60–90 days before transacting — owners cutting to S$1,400–1,550 psf to move.
ABSD pressure on upgraders means fewer competitive bids; sellers who wait beyond 90 days carry all the holding cost.

slides.label: The real split
slides.punch: Developers hold ASP because they can. Resale sellers hold hope — until month three, when bills arrive and bids don't.
slides.punch_bold: The buyer with cash and patience is the only one not blinking.

slides.pre: New landed ASP — D10 / D11 launches, Apr 2026
slides.post: Developer pricing barely moved — new stock anchoring at S$2,050 psf median, resale trails by S$500–650 psf.
slides.source: URA caveats · developer price lists · Apr 2026

slides.pre: Eyeing landed in D10 / D15 / D19?
slides.title: Let's map your
slides.sub: 30-min strategy call. I'll show you exactly where the discount is real and where it's a trap — no pitch, just data.

slides.title: Forward this to
slides.sub: If you know anyone weighing new vs resale landed right now, send them this before they sign anything.

caption:
Brand new vs resale landed — the gap is widening in April 2026. 🏠

• Resale sellers are absorbing ~5% discounts to last ask, widest since mid-2023
• New launches in D10/D11 holding firm at ~S$2,050 psf — developers not blinking
• D19 resale terraces sitting ~91 days before transacting; D11 moves in ~34

If you're buying, patience is your edge. If you're selling resale, day 90 is the cliff.
